steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence sets up a logical inference: neither Geoffrey's History nor any work predating it mentions the Round Table, yet Malory's Le Morte d'Arthur does. The blank must complete the logical "suggesting that ______" conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Geoffrey's accounts are more similar to Culhwch and Olwen than to Le Morte d'Arthur": the passage never compares the content similarity of these texts, so this is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — "when an Arthurian legend contradicted History, Malory preferred Geoffrey's version": the passage says Malory compiled contradictory sources, not that he favored Geoffrey's version over others. ✗
  • Choice C — "Le Morte d'Arthur is more historically accurate because Culhwch and Olwen hadn't been written when Geoffrey was writing": historical accuracy is never discussed, and the timeline claim doesn't follow from the Round Table evidence. ✗
  • Choice D — "Malory encountered the Round Table in a source that Geoffrey was not familiar with": directly follows from the evidence. The Round Table is absent from History and all earlier works, so Malory must have drawn it from a source outside Geoffrey's sphere — consistent with the note that only many (not all) of Malory's sources derive from Geoffrey. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
